chosen_title: ITR filing 2026: 6 costly mistakes salaried taxpayers should avoid before 31 July deadline
caption:
Your Form 16 arrived. Your employer deducted TDS. You think you're sorted — you're not.

July 31 is 30 days away. The IT dept's AIS already holds your FD interest, dividends, mutual fund redemptions, and more. An ITR that doesn't match what the dept sees invites a notice — automatically.

Six mistakes hitting salaried filers right now:
① Wrong ITR form — capital gains or two employers? Don't use ITR-1.
② AIS unchecked — cross-verify every entry before accepting pre-filled data.
③ FD/savings interest not declared — banks report it; the dept sees it.
④ 'TDS done = tax done' is wrong — all other income must still be declared.
⑤ Regime chosen by habit — calculate both; the saving gap can exceed ₹30,000.
⑥ Filing after July 31 — ₹5,000 penalty, even if you're owed a refund.

Illustration: Amit (₹10L salary, 30% bracket) forgot to declare ₹80,000 FD interest. Tax due: ₹24,000. Under-reporting penalty at 50%: ₹12,000. Total surprise bill: ₹36,000 — from one missed line. (Numbers illustrative; actual liability depends on your regime and deductions.)

What to do: Download your AIS at incometax.gov.in → match every entry against your documents → then file.

Need it done right before the deadline? WhatsApp us — ITR from ₹999, filed clean and on time.

Note: general information, not professional advice — talk to us for your specific case.

Source: Income Tax Act, Sections 234F, 270A; incometax.gov.in AIS portal
